headline: Attackers turned the defenders' own gear against them, exploiting Fortinet's FortiSandbox and Cisco's SD-WAN Manager as CISA stacked fresh patch deadlines and a ransomware crew hid inside Microsoft Teams.

emerging_trends.subject: Tools as Targets
emerging_trends.text: Attackers exploited FortiSandbox and Cisco SD-WAN Manager this week, turning the appliances meant to enforce defense into the way in.

emerging_trends.subject: Trusted-Platform Abuse
emerging_trends.text: Malware rode legitimate services—Microsoft Teams relays, Steam Workshop, the JetBrains Marketplace, and the Arch User Repository—to blend command traffic and delivery into normal noise.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Attack Surface
emerging_trends.text: Flaws in Microsoft Copilot, the JetBrains plugin ecosystem, and Google Vertex AI widened what attackers can steal from the AI tooling developers now lean on daily.

emerging_trends.subject: Identity Mandates
emerging_trends.text: Britain moved to require ID or a face scan for new social-media accounts while India blocked Telegram over exam-cheating fraud, tying online access ever closer to verified identity.

topics.title: Attackers Exploit FortiSandbox Threat Platform
topics.latest_developments: Defused logged attacks against three FortiSandbox flaws—CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, and CVE-2026-25089—inside 24 hours, one of them patched only last week, and judged one of the exploits vibe-coded and likely faulty.
topics.summary: FortiSandbox renders threat verdicts that other Fortinet products trust to block traffic and trigger automated responses, so compromising it blinds an entire defensive stack. CVE-2026-39813, a path-traversal flaw in the JRPC API, rates 9.1. Organizations running FortiSandbox should apply Fortinet's fixes now and hunt for forged verdicts or unexpected API calls.

sources.title: Attackers are exploiting FortiSandbox vulnerabilities
Attackers Exploit Three Fortinet FortiSandbox Flaws, One Patched Last Week
Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws now exploited in attacks

topics.title: CISA Sets New Deadlines for Exploited Web Flaws
topics.latest_developments: CISA added Joomla Content Editor flaw CVE-2026-48907 to its known exploited catalog, gave agencies until June 18 to patch LiteSpeed cPanel plugin flaw CVE-2026-54420, and Cisco shipped fixes for Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaw CVE-2026-20262, which it found in internal testing yet attackers reached first.
topics.summary: All three flaws give attackers a foothold on internet-facing infrastructure: Joomla content editing, LiteSpeed's cPanel plugin at root, and Cisco's SD-WAN management plane. CVE-2026-54420 rates 8.5 and enables root privilege escalation. Federal civilian agencies face binding deadlines, and private operators of the same software should treat the timelines as their own.

sources.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
CISA warns of another cPanel plugin flaw exploited in attacks
Cisco discloses second exploited SD-WAN vulnerability in two weeks (CVE-2026-20262)

topics.title: Atomic Arch Attack Swells to 1,500 Packages
topics.latest_developments: The Atomic supply-chain campaign against the Arch User Repository grew to roughly 1,500 malicious packages, and Arch Linux suspended new account registrations to stem the upload wave.
topics.summary: Attackers rewrote build scripts across AUR packages to ship a Rust infostealer and an eBPF rootkit, expanding from the roughly 400 packages reported earlier in the week. The community repository lets any registered user publish, which made it easy to flood with tainted entries. Arch users should audit recently installed AUR packages, rebuild from clean sources, and assume credentials touched on affected systems are exposed.

sources.title: Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages

topics.title: DragonForce Hides C2 Inside Microsoft Teams
topics.latest_developments: Symantec caught the DragonForce ransomware group routing command-and-control traffic through Microsoft Teams TURN relay infrastructure with custom malware called Backdoor.Turn during an intrusion at a U.S. services company, the first known abuse of Teams relays for this purpose.
topics.summary: DragonForce runs a ransomware-as-a-service operation active since 2023, arming affiliates for a cut of payments. By tunneling traffic through Teams relays, the group disguises its command channel as routine collaboration traffic that defenders rarely block. Security teams should scrutinize Teams TURN connections and treat unexplained relay traffic as a possible hiding place for malware.

sources.title: Cybercriminals mask malicious communications through Microsoft Teams relays
Ransomware gang abuses Microsoft Teams relays to hide malicious traffic

topics.title: Security Experts Fight the Anthropic Export Ban
topics.latest_developments: Dozens of security professionals signed an open letter pressing Washington to reverse its order blocking export of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and Wired argued models with advanced hacking ability will soon be common regardless of the restriction.
topics.summary: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals, pulling both models worldwide on June 13 and framing the move as export control over offensive AI capability. Signatories call the cited jailbreak narrow and the underlying ability already widespread, warning the ban hampers defenders more than attackers. The fight sets an early precedent for how governments treat frontier models that can find and exploit software flaws.

sources.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
'Dangerous' AI Models Are Coming No Matter What

topics.title: AI Coding Tools Bleed Keys and 2FA Codes
topics.latest_developments: Researchers disclosed SearchLeak, a critical Microsoft Copilot flaw that let attackers steal users' two-factor authentication codes, and BleepingComputer found at least 15 malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins harvesting developers' AI API keys.
topics.summary: SearchLeak shows how prompt and retrieval features can be turned to exfiltrate sensitive data the assistant can see, including login codes. The rogue JetBrains plugins target the API keys developers store for their AI services, handing attackers paid access and a route into projects. Developers should remove untrusted plugins, rotate exposed keys, and treat AI assistants as systems that handle secrets.

sources.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to steal 2FA code from users
Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers

topics.title: Health and Sports Data Spills in Fresh Hacks
topics.latest_developments: iRhythm confirmed hackers stole patient personal and health data from third-party-hosted apps and demanded a ransom, the FulcrumSec group claimed it lifted 1.3TB from Novo Nordisk, and attackers published Madison Square Garden and New York Knicks data, including a risk-rated list of celebrities and players.
topics.summary: iRhythm, a cardiac-monitoring firm, learned of its breach on June 8 and traced the loss to business applications hosted by an outside provider. The Novo Nordisk and MSG dumps show extortion crews chasing both regulated health records and embarrassing internal files. Affected customers and patients should expect targeted phishing and watch for fraud tied to the stolen records.

sources.title: iRhythm discloses data breach, says hackers stole patient info
Cybercrime Group Claims Novo Nordisk Hack
Hackers Publish Knicks and Madison Square Garden Data Online

topics.title: ScarCruft Drops NarwhalRAT via Fake Microsoft Alerts
topics.latest_developments: Genians Security Center reported North Korea's ScarCruft, also tracked as APT37, sending spear-phishing emails that impersonate Microsoft account security notifications to deploy a new remote-access trojan called NarwhalRAT.
topics.summary: ScarCruft is a long-running North Korean espionage group that favors social-engineering lures aimed at researchers, defectors, and officials. The fake security alerts play on recipients' fear that their accounts are at risk, prompting the click that installs NarwhalRAT. Defenders should flag emails impersonating Microsoft account warnings and verify any such notice through the account portal rather than email links.

sources.title: Fake Microsoft Alerts Used to Deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT Malware

weather: Tonight: Mostly Cloudy then Chance Rain Showers, low 59F.
Wednesday Night: Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 68F.

games.recap: Nick Kurtz and Jeff McNeil power the A's to an 11-2 victory over the struggling Pirates

business_politics.title: Bank of Japan Raises Rate to 1%
business_politics.latest_developments: The Bank of Japan lifted its policy rate to 1% on Tuesday, a step beyond the central-bank moves tracked in prior briefings and a milestone in its long exit from near-zero rates.
business_politics.summary: Japan's central bank pushed its benchmark to 1%, the highest in roughly two decades, as it normalizes policy after years of ultra-loose settings. The move firms global yields and reshapes the carry trade that funnels Japanese capital abroad.

sources.title: FirstFT: Oil falls below $80 as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return

business_politics.title: Iran Deal Lets Tehran Sell Oil at Once
business_politics.latest_developments: Terms emerged Tuesday showing the U.S.-Iran agreement lets Tehran sell oil immediately and waives banking and transport sanctions, an early financial benefit beyond the signing reported earlier.
business_politics.summary: The accord ending the war restores Iran's access to oil markets and the financial system needed to transact, hastening a supply recovery. Brent crude fell below $80 for a fourth straight session as traders priced the return of Strait of Hormuz flows.

sources.title: The Trump-Iran Deal Allows Tehran to Immediately Sell Oil
Oil sinks below $80 as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return

business.title: Skill Games Ruling May Spur Legislation
business.latest_developments: Officials say Monday's state Supreme Court ruling that skill games must follow gaming law could finally push Harrisburg lawmakers to regulate and tax the machines.
business.summary: Pennsylvania's high court held that the loosely regulated skill games in bars, convenience stores, and clubs count as slot machines under state law. Lawmakers have stalled for years on rules and taxes for the devices, and officials see the decision as the spark to act.

sources.title: Pa. court ruling could spur legislative action on skill games, officials say

business.title: Steel Industry Marks Its Legacy
business.latest_developments: WTAE profiled the Mon Valley Works and the workers who keep Pittsburgh's steelmaking tradition alive, framing the industry's role in building modern America.
business.summary: Pittsburgh's steel mills shaped skyscrapers, bridges, and wartime production for more than a century, and the Mon Valley Works still runs today. The piece centers on the pride of current workers carrying a generations-old trade.

sources.title: Pittsburgh steel industry celebrates its role in building America

around_town.title: Fern Hollow Bridge Settlement
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh City Council is reviewing a proposed $445,000 settlement for nine people hurt when the Fern Hollow Bridge collapsed on January 28, 2022.
around_town.summary: The Forbes Avenue bridge over Frick Park fell on a snowy morning, dropping vehicles and a bus into the ravine. The proposed payout would resolve claims from nine victims, with council weighing approval.

sources.title: Council reviews more than $400K settlement for Fern Hollow Bridge collapse victims

around_town.title: Tornado Count Rises to Five
around_town.latest_developments: The National Weather Service confirmed two more tornadoes in Butler County, including an EF1 near Moraine State Park, raising Sunday's regional total to five across eastern Ohio and northwestern Pennsylvania.
around_town.summary: Sunday's line of severe storms spawned multiple twisters, with surveys still ongoing. Earlier confirmations covered Beaver County and Columbiana County, Ohio; the Butler County additions bring the count to five.

sources.title: 5 tornadoes confirmed across parts of northwestern Pennsylvania and eastern Ohio following Sunday storms

around_town.title: Free Summer Meals for Kids
around_town.latest_developments: CitiParks reopened its free summer meal program Tuesday, offering breakfast, lunch, and snacks to any child under 18 through mid-August.
around_town.summary: As Pittsburgh schools close, the city, Pittsburgh Public Schools, and the Greater Pittsburgh Community Food Bank serve free meals at eight rec centers and more than 40 partner sites. No registration is required for children under 18.

sources.title: Pittsburgh continues free summer meal program for children under 18

around_town.title: State Funds Security for South Side Fest
around_town.latest_developments: Pennsylvania granted $125,000 to the South Side Community Action Network to hire private security this summer for the adults-only South Side Street Fest along East Carson Street.
around_town.summary: East Carson Street anchors much of Pittsburgh's nightlife, and the network runs a summer festival there. The state money pays private guards to supplement city police at the event.

sources.title: Private security to supplement Pittsburgh cops at South Side festival

around_town.title: Mt. Oliver Internet Restored
around_town.latest_developments: Xfinity restored service Tuesday to Mt. Oliver customers on Margaret Street after a near-48-hour outage that cut residents off from remote work.
around_town.summary: The blackout left a borough block disconnected for two days, stranding people who clock in for jobs online. Comcast brought the connection back Tuesday.

sources.title: Internet restored to Xfinity customers in Mt. Oliver after 48-hour blackout

events.title: Juneteenth Events Across the Region
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette rounded up Pittsburgh's Juneteenth observances for the June 19 holiday, spanning festivals, film screenings, and more.
events.summary: Juneteenth, Friday, June 19, 2026, marks the end of slavery in the United States. Pittsburgh hosts a slate of festivals, films, and gatherings around the date; see the guide for locations and times.

sources.title: Juneteenth events in Pittsburgh include festivals, films and more

around_teams.title: Kyler Fedko's MLB Debut
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette featured Pirates prospect Kyler Fedko making his major-league debut, watched from the stands by his father, Pittsburgh broadcaster John Fedko.
around_teams.summary: Fedko reached the majors with the Pirates, a milestone the paper framed through his proud father's presence. The piece runs as a human-interest look at the call-up.

sources.title: Helicopter dad? A proud John Fedko was there to watch his son, Kyler, make his MLB debut

around_teams.title: SNR Drive on QB Tiers, Offseason Moves
around_teams.latest_developments: On Tuesday's SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler ran through league news, broke down Evan Silva's 2026 fantasy quarterback tiers, and weighed the best offseason moves and longest playoff droughts.
around_teams.summary: The Steelers' SNR Drive is a daily team-channel show with analysts Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler. The June 16 episode mixed minicamp storylines with a fantasy-football quarterback ranking.

sources.title: News Of The Day + Discussing Fantasy Football QB Tiers | SNR Drive (June.16) | Pittsburgh Steelers

around_teams.title: Which Steelers Rookies Contribute Early
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette video assessed which Steelers rookies looked ready to contribute after OTAs and minicamp, singling out receiver Germie Bernard and offensive lineman Max Iheanachor.
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh wrapped its spring program, giving beat writers a first read on the draft class. The video weighs which newcomers could earn early roles.

sources.title: WATCH: Are any Steelers rookies looking like quick contributors after OTAs, minicamp?

around_teams.title: Termarr Johnson Trending Up
around_teams.latest_developments: MiLB Monday in the Post-Gazette asked whether former top Pirates pick Termarr Johnson is turning his minor-league season around.
around_teams.summary: Johnson, a high first-round selection, has struggled to develop in the Pirates system. The column tracks recent signs of improvement among him and other prospects.

sources.title: MiLB Monday: Is former top Pirates pick Termarr Johnson turning things around?

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports figures showing OpenAI's losses ballooned almost eightfold in 2025 on $34 billion of spending, arguing the company has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fox Buys Roku, The Problem With Fox’s Smart Strategy, Streaming That Works
reading.summary: Thompson argues Fox's purchase of Roku trades extraction from rights holders for the weaker leverage of a distribution renter, explaining why the market dislikes the deal.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Anthropic’s Safety Superpower
reading.summary: Thompson contends Anthropic's faith in its own safety commitment licenses it to push its business aggressively and even challenge the U.S. government.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn’t Breaking Work. It’s Already Broken.
reading.summary: Newport uses a survey of 6,000 digital workers to argue that AI exposes dysfunction already baked into knowledge work rather than creating it.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
